ddls:
CREATE TABLE "power_plants" (
  "plant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each power plant */,
  "plant_name" TEXT /* Name of the power plant */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the power plant */,
  "capacity" REAL /* Capacity of the power plant in MW */,
  "commission_date" TEXT /* Date when the plant was commissioned */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last major maintenance */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current operational status */,
  "fuel_type" TEXT /* Type of fuel used */,
  "environmental_rating" TEXT /* Environmental rating of the plant */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("plant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of the sensor (e.g., temperature, pressure, flow rate) */,
  "plant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the power plant where the sensor is installed */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the sensor was installed */,
  "last_calibration_date" TEXT /* Date of the last calibration */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current operational status */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the sensor */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the sensor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensors_plant_id FOREIGN KEY ("plant_id") REFERENCES power_plants ("plant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "measurements" (
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that collected the measurement */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the measurement */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the measurement */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement */,
  "sensor_status" TEXT /* Status of the sensor at the time of measurement */,
  "measurement_type" TEXT /* Type of measurement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurements_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "machines" (
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each machine */,
  "machine_name" TEXT /* Name of the machine */,
  "machine_type" TEXT /* Type of the machine (e.g., turbine, pump, generator) */,
  "plant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the power plant where the machine is installed */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the machine was installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current operational status */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the machine */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the machine */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the machine */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("machine_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_machines_plant_id FOREIGN KEY ("plant_id") REFERENCES power_plants ("plant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* ID of the machine that the metric belongs to */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the metric */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of performance metric */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the metric */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the machine at the time of the metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_machine_id FOREIGN KEY ("machine_id") REFERENCES machines ("machine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "emissions" (
  "emission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each emission */,
  "plant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the power plant that the emission belongs to */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the emission */,
  "nox" REAL /* NOx emission in kg/h */,
  "co2" REAL /* CO2 emission in kg/h */,
  "so2" REAL /* SO2 emission in kg/h */,
  "pm10" REAL /* PM10 emission in kg/h */,
  "pm25" REAL /* PM2.5 emission in kg/h */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the plant at the time of emission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("emission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_emissions_plant_id FOREIGN KEY ("plant_id") REFERENCES power_plants ("plant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance record */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* ID of the machine that the maintenance belongs to */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the maintenance */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance */,
  "duration" REAL /* Duration of the maintenance in hours */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost of the maintenance */,
  "performed_by" TEXT /* Name of the person or team who performed the maintenance */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_machine_id FOREIGN KEY ("machine_id") REFERENCES machines ("machine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction */,
  "plant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the power plant that the prediction belongs to */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the prediction */,
  "predicted_value" REAL /* Predicted value */,
  "model_used" TEXT /* Model used for the prediction */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Confidence level of the prediction */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the prediction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_plant_id FOREIGN KEY ("plant_id") REFERENCES power_plants ("plant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, operator, analyst) */,
  "date_joined" TEXT /* Date when the user joined */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user that the log belongs to */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the log */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user (e.g., login, logout, view data) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent of the user's browser */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
